Journal.Title: Huan jing ke xue= Huanjing kexue

Article.ArticleTitle: [Impacts of Land Use Transformation on Ecological Environmental Quality in Coastal Zone of East China Sea Under Perspective of "Production-living-ecology Spaces"].

Abstract.AbstractText: We provide a basis for regional land spatial planning and ecological environmental construction to explore the patterns of ecological environmental quality changes in coastal zone of East China Sea. We investigate the impact of land use transformation on ecological environmental quality using four research methods (land use transfer matrix, ecological environment quality, ecological contribution rate of land transformation, and Geodetector) on the land transformation characteristics of "production-living-ecology spaces" from 2000 to 2020. The findings indicated that: ① Land use transformation had a significant and deep impact on the ecological environment quality of coastal zone of East China Sea. It was primarily dominated by production and ecological land use. The area of agricultural land has been continuously decreasing, while the areas of residential and ecological land have been continuously expanding from 2000 to 2020. ② The ecological environment showed an overall slight downward trend in coastal zone of East China Sea. The ecological environmental quality index in this region remained relatively high but displayed a slight decline over time from 2000 to 2020. Spatially, higher values were observed in inland areas, while lower values were found in the northern plains and coastal areas. This indicated considerable internal variability in ecological environmental quality. ③ The interaction of multiple factors significantly impacted the ecological environmental quality of coastal zone of East China Sea. Key drivers included land use intensity, slope, and terrain undulation, with the combined effects of these factors being greater than the influence of any single factor on ecological environmental quality.
